title: Agent Receipt
description: A cryptographically signed record of a single action taken by an AI agent on behalf of a human principal. Conforms to the Agent Receipt Protocol v0.1.

@context.description: JSON-LD context. First two entries MUST be the W3C VC v2 and Attest context URIs in order. Additional context URIs MAY follow.

id.description: Globally unique receipt identifier. MUST be a urn:receipt:<uuid>.

version.description: Protocol version. MUST be "0.1.0", "0.2.0", or "0.2.1". Verifiers MUST accept all three.

issuanceDate.description: ISO 8601 datetime when the receipt was issued. MAY differ from action.timestamp in batched scenarios. Uses the VC 1.x field name; see ADR-0009 for the rationale.

sha256Hash.description: SHA-256 digest in the format sha256:<64 hex chars>.

id.description: DID or URI identifying the agent instance.
DID or URI of the entity operating the agent.
DID or URI identifying the principal.
Unique action identifier. MUST be act_<uuid>.

type.description: Action type from the taxonomy (§5). Dot-separated hierarchical identifier.

risk_level.description: Risk classification. Implementations MAY escalate but MUST NOT downgrade from taxonomy defaults.

system.description: The system or service acted upon.

resource.description: The specific resource within the system.

parameters_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the action parameters (RFC 8785 canonical JSON).

parameters_disclosure.description: Operator-controlled flat map of parameter field name → stringified value. Only fields the operator allowlists via the `parameterDisclosure` config (per ADR-0012) are ever included. The parameters_hash still covers the full parameter set; the disclosure is additive metadata for human/auditor display. The envelope-based encrypted form is reserved for a future schema version per ADR-0012.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 datetime when the action was executed.

trusted_timestamp.description: Base64-encoded RFC 3161 TimeStampToken (DER encoding). Absent when not present; MUST NOT be null.

conversation_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the conversation context.

prompt_preview.description: Plain-text preview of the prompt that triggered the action (may be truncated).

reasoning_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the agent's reasoning trace.

error.description: Error message if status is "failure". Absent when not applicable; MUST NOT be null.

reversible.description: true if the action can be undone.

reversal_window_seconds.description: Seconds within which reversal is possible.

reversal_of.description: id of the original receipt that this receipt reverses. Present only on reversal receipts.

before_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of relevant state before the action.

after_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of relevant state after the action.

response_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the RFC 8785 canonical JSON of the server's response, computed after secret redaction (redact → hash → sign). Absent when the issuer did not commit to the response.

scopes.description: Authorization scopes active at time of action.

granted_at.description: ISO 8601 datetime when authorization was granted.

grant_ref.description: Reference to the authorization grant (e.g. a Grantex grant token). Absent when not applicable; MUST NOT be null.

delegation.description: Present when this chain was spawned by delegation from another agent.

parent_chain_id.description: chain_id of the delegating agent's chain.

parent_receipt_id.description: id of the receipt in the parent chain where delegation occurred.

id.description: DID or URI of the delegating agent (the parent chain's issuer).

sequence.description: Monotonically increasing position within the chain. Starts at 1.

previous_receipt_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the previous receipt's canonical form. MUST be null for the first receipt (sequence: 1).

chain_id.description: Opaque identifier grouping receipts into a logical chain.

terminal.description: When present, asserts this is the final receipt in the chain. MUST be true if present; absence means no claim. Explicit false is not a valid value — the schema restricts this field to the constant true.

created.description: ISO 8601 datetime when the proof was created.

verificationMethod.description: DID URL of the signing key (e.g. "did:agent:...#key-1").

proofValue.description: Multibase-encoded (u-prefixed base64url, no padding) Ed25519 signature over the canonical receipt (proof field excluded).
